observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management
agreed that the:
(a) LCE (i) reconstitute the Inventory Committee to provide
adequate number of personnel to be able to prepare the Physical
Inventory Plan and complete the physical inventory in adherence
with COA Circular 2020-006, by following the general and
procedural guidelines stated in the said Circular including disposition
and recording of the missing PPEs; and
(ii) request for authority for the derecognition of missing items,
duly supported with appropriate documentation, if
accountability/responsibility over the missing PPE could not be
pinpointed after investigation as required under Section 7 of COA
Circular 2020-006;
(b) Management allocate fund for the expenses to secure copies of the
title of the declared real properties and assign a focal person who will
focus on the requirements needed to facilitate the immediate transfer
of titles of the parcels of land under the name of the Municipality;
(c) Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant request and
assign, as the case may be, personnel who will prepare and maintain
updated property cards to enable reconciliation with the general
ledger balances and the inventory report;
(d) Municipal Accountant to
(i) semi-expendable property which were recognized as PPE and
reclassify to the affected appropriate semi-expendable inventory
accounts (if not yet issued to end-user), expense accounts (if
issued within the year) or necessary accumulated
surplus/deficit/retained earnings accounts (if issued in prior years)
consistent with Section 5.4 of COA Circular No. 2016-006; and
(ii) effect the necessary adjusting entries for the misclassified
accounts in accordance with Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs
and the Manual on New Government Accounting System;
(e) Designated General Services Officer to
(i) work for the registration and to secure insurance coverage for all
government motor vehicles owned by the Municipality, including
donated vehicles; and for donated vehicles yet to be transferred to the
name of the LGU.
(ii) prepare motor vehicle profile indicating the date of acquisition,
name of the model/make/type and cost to determine those which have
to be protected with comprehensive insurance; and
(f) Municipal Treasurer together with the Designated General Services
Officer with the assistance of the Inventory Committee to consider
undertaking a one-time cleansing of PPE account balances and
strictly comply with the guidelines and procedures enumerated in
COA Circular No. 2020-006.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to –
a) comply with the registration requirements with regard to the operation of the
constructed concrete vault located in the centralized MRF and the required training of
personnel who will handle such wastes in order to fully maximize its use;
b) strictly
(i) comply with Section 48 (3) of RA No. 9003; and (ii) implement its Municipal
Ordinance in relation to open burning of solid waste; and
c) continue its efforts and initiatives in implementing innovative plans and programs
incorporated in its 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan in accordance with RA No.
9003.
observations.managementAction:
All personnel were determined in continuing its efforts and initiatives in implementing
innovative plans incorporated in our 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan.
Municipal Ordinance No. 23-109 pertaining to open burning of solid wastes has been
communicated to all Municipal personnel to ensure their compliance.
The LGU will also strictly observe and monitor our jurisdiction in order to enforce the
prohibition of burning of solid wastes as what Section 48, RA No. 9003 or the Ecological
Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 and Municipal Ordinance No. 23-109 state.
